subject: All Classes
assignment_purpose: Prove classroom toolkit layout_template_id routes through the normal render:package pipeline.
success_criteria: Each page renders through outputs[] using an existing output type.
The toolkit layouts keep clear writing space and print-friendly structure.

kwhl_chart.title: KWHL Chart
kwhl_chart.subtitle: Know — Want to know — How will I find out — Learned
kwhl_chart.directions: Before: Fill in K and W. During: Add to H as you discover sources or strategies. After: Complete the L column.
kwhl_chart.learning_target: I can organize what I know, what I wonder, how I will learn, and what I learned.
checklist: K and W were started before the lesson.
H includes sources, strategies, or people I can ask.
L is written in my own words.

fishbone_diagram.title: Fishbone Diagram
fishbone_diagram.subtitle: Identify the causes that lead to a problem, event, or outcome.
fishbone_diagram.directions: Write the problem or effect in the box. Label each cause category, then fill in specific causes.
fishbone_diagram.tip: Ask why this happened for each cause you write.
checklist: The problem or effect is clear.
Each category has at least one specific cause.
I identified a likely root cause.

sentence_frame_card.title: Sentence Frame Card
sentence_frame_card.subtitle: Use these frames to explain thinking, discuss respectfully, and write responses.

choice_board.title: Choice Board
choice_board.subtitle: Pick your tasks. Show what you know in a way that works for you.
choice_board.directions: Choose 3 tasks that connect like a line — across, down, or diagonal.
choice_board.learning_target: I can show my understanding through a task pathway that matches my strengths.
checklist: I checked off each finished task.
I can explain my strongest choice.

scaffolded_quiz.title: Low-Stakes Unit Check
scaffolded_quiz.subtitle: Scaffolded support with word bank and hints
scaffolded_quiz.support_label: Scaffolded Support — word bank & hints provided
scaffolded_quiz.learning_target: I can show what I know using vocabulary and complete explanations.

multiple_choice.question: What should evidence do in a response?
A strong reflection usually includes
options: What I learned and what I need next

short_answer.question: Explain one strategy that helps you learn difficult material.
short_answer.hint: Name the strategy and explain how it helps.

short_answer.question: Use one word from the word bank in a complete sentence.
short_answer.hint: Make the meaning clear from the sentence.
